risks: No managed identity configured — Container App uses key-based secrets for Event Grid and Cosmos DB, increasing credential exposure risk
All resources use public network access — suitable for development but not production-sensitive workloads
Cosmos DB serverless is single-region only — no failover or disaster recovery capability
No health probes (liveness, readiness, startup) configured on the Container App — unhealthy instances may receive traffic
No Key Vault integration — secrets are stored directly in Container App configuration rather than referenced from Key Vault
No custom KEDA scaling rule for Event Grid events — scale-to-zero relies on default scaling, not event-driven triggers
correctionsRecommended: Add a user-assigned managed identity to the Container App and use Azure RBAC for Cosmos DB and Event Grid access instead of key-based secrets
Store secrets in Azure Key Vault and reference them via Key Vault secret URIs in the Container App and Dapr component configuration
Configure liveness, readiness, and startup health probes on the Container App per WAF recommendations (e.g., failureThreshold: 3, periodSeconds: 10)
Add a custom scaling rule based on Event Grid or Dapr pub/sub metrics for true event-driven autoscaling
Consider disabling public network access on Cosmos DB and upgrading Event Grid to Premium SKU with private endpoints for production workloads
Add Cosmos DB database and container child resources (Microsoft.DocumentDB/databaseAccounts/sqlDatabases) for complete infrastructure definition
Enable CORS policy on the Container App ingress configuration per Azure IaC best practices
